title: Introduction to Social Welfare Practice
catalogDescription: Introduction to the field of social work, including the theoretical concepts and institutional framework that guide practice. Overview of social work profession and social welfare system within which it operates. Lectures supplemented by exercises, films, guest lectures, and class discussions. Offered: A.

title: Orientation to Social Work
catalogDescription: Seminar includes a range of workshops, activities, guest speakers, and panels geared toward orienting students to the UW social work program and the profession of social work. Credit/no-credit only. Offered: A.

title: Social Welfare Practice I
catalogDescription: Provides an introduction to the roles, tasks, and functions of the social welfare practitioner and to theories and methods of intervention; a conceptual framework for social work practice with individuals, families, and small groups; and an opportunity to develop skills in problem assessment, intervention, termination, and evaluation. Course equivalent to: TSOCWF 310. Offered: A.

title: Social Welfare Practice II
catalogDescription: Provides an introduction to the roles, tasks, and functions of the social welfare practitioner and to theories and methods of intervention; a conceptual framework for social work practice with individuals, families, and small groups; and an opportunity to develop skills in problem assessment, intervention, termination, and evaluation. Prerequisite: SOC WF 310. Offered: W.

title: Social Welfare Practice III
catalogDescription: Focus on macro systems in a diverse society using the generalist perspective. The implications of system resources and configurations for meeting human needs are considered. The role and function of generalist social workers to understand and advocate for system development and change is emphasized. Course equivalent to: TSOCWF 312. Prerequisite: SOC WF 311. Offered: Sp.
prerequisiteNotes: Official UW prerequisite text: SOC WF 311.

title: Introduction to Group Work Practice
catalogDescription: Focuses on developing an understanding of both group dynamics and effective leadership and facilitation skills with groups. Students learn about group work from theoretical and empirical literature, as well as intervention models relevant to social work practice. Prerequisite: SOC WF 312. Offered: W.

title: Social Welfare Policy
catalogDescription: Emphasizes policy and program development in social welfare with emphasis on the context, making, and unmaking of social policy. Covers policy formulation as well as current and emerging policies in social welfare. Maximum 10 credits allowed between TSOCWF 300; TSOCWF 320; and SOC WF 320. Prerequisite: SOC WF 200. Offered: Sp.

title: Introduction to Social Welfare Research
catalogDescription: Introduction to logic of the scientific method as applied to social work and social welfare practice, to the design and conduct of a research study, and to data collection and summarization. Skill development in critical consumption of social welfare research. Course equivalent to: TSOCWF 390. Course overlaps with: T CRIM 390. Prerequisite: either EDPSY 490, QMETH 201, STAT 220, STAT 221/CS&SS 221/SOC 221, STAT 290, STAT 390, or STAT 311. Offered: A.
prerequisiteNotes: Official UW prerequisite text: either EDPSY 490, QMETH 201, STAT 220, STAT 221/CS&SS 221/SOC 221, STAT 290, STAT 390, or STAT 311.

title: Human Behavior and Social Environment I
catalogDescription: Focuses on understanding human development across the lifespan. Integrates biological, psychological, structural, environmental, political, global, and socio-cultural perspectives. Explores the relationship between the person and the environment including families, groups, organizations, communities, and institutions. Course equivalent to: TSOCWF 402.

title: Cultural Diversity and Justice
catalogDescription: History and culture of disadvantaged and oppressed groups served by social welfare generalist practitioners. Course equivalent to: TSOCWF 404. Offered: W.

title: Fieldwork Seminar
catalogDescription: Integrates social work practicum experiences with prior and concurrent coursework in social sciences, social work, and research. Includes discussion of class presentations and simulations or practice situations that combine knowledge and skill utilization. Student logs provide a basis for individual goal identification and achievement. Required of social welfare seniors. Course overlaps with: TSOCWF 405. Offered: A.

title: Beginning Field Instruction
catalogDescription: Students are placed in selected social service agencies and accept beginning social service assignments under the supervision of competent agency personnel. Course overlaps with: TSOCWF 415. Credit/no-credit only. Offered: AWSp.

title: Skills for Social Welfare Practice
catalogDescription: Builds on first-year generalist practice content. Provides skill building in one or two areas of social work practice relevant to many practice settings. Topic chosen by students and faculty. Credit/no-credit only. Offered: W.

title: Chemical Dependency: Assessment and Treatment
catalogDescription: Provides students the knowledge and basic skills needed to recognize and understand the dynamics of chemical dependency in the lives of individuals. Students learn how to assess the presence of this disorder and what the issues are in referring individuals to treatment. Offered: Sp.

title: Social Welfare Capstone Seminar
catalogDescription: Students demonstrate and reflect on mastery of core competencies and respective practice behaviors foundational to generalist practice. Studies analyze and synthesize data from their experiences in the BASW program and integrate this understanding with their new identity as a professional social worker. Credit/no-credit only. Offered: Sp.
